prompt: The stock calculation is wrong: items with multiple warehouse locations show double the actual quantity

turns.content:
<task>
The stock calculation is wrong: items with multiple warehouse locations show double the actual quantity
</task>

tests/test_stock.py::test_total_stock_single_location PASSED
tests/test_stock.py::test_total_stock_multi_location FAILED

    def test_total_stock_multi_location():
        item = Item("Widget", sku="W001")
        item.add_stock("warehouse_a", 50)
        item.add_stock("warehouse_b", 30)
>       assert item.total_stock() == 80
E       assert 160 == 80

20:     def add_stock(self, location: str, quantity: int):
21:         if location in self.locations:
22:             self.locations[location] += quantity
23:         else:
24:             self.locations[location] = quantity
25:         self._total_cache = None
26: 
27:     def total_stock(self) -> int:
28:         if self._total_cache is None:
29:             total = sum(self.locations.values())
30:             self._total_cache = total
31:         return self._total_cache + sum(self.locations.values())

arguments.reasoning: Found it. Line 31: `return self._total_cache + sum(self.locations.values())`. It returns the cached total PLUS another sum of all locations. It should just return self._total_cache. The cache is correctly computed on line 29, but line 31 adds the values again.

arguments.summary: Found bug at line 31: total_stock() returns `self._total_cache + sum(self.locations.values())` which doubles the count. Should return just `self._total_cache`.

next_steps.description: Fix line 31: change return to just self._total_cache

arguments.summary: Fixed double-counting bug in total_stock(): line 31 was adding sum(locations.values()) to the already-computed cache. Changed to return just self._total_cache. Both tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed stock double-counting in src/inventory/models.py line 31. total_stock() was returning `self._total_cache + sum(locations.values())`, doubling the result. Changed to return just `self._total_cache`. Both tests pass.
